mnemonics: AUNT: For All (Universal) quantifier, think 'AUNT' - All UNiversal is a upside down A. For Exists (Existential) quantifier, think 'E' for 'Exists' or 'There is at least one'.
keyPoints: A universal quantifier (∀) means 'for all' elements in a domain, the statement is true.
An existential quantifier (∃) means 'there exists' at least one element in the domain for which the statement is true.
The order of quantifiers matters; ∀x∃y is not logically equivalent to ∃y∀x.
commonMistakes: Assuming the order of mixed quantifiers (like ∀∃ vs. ∃∀) doesn't change the meaning of the statement.
Incorrectly negating quantified statements, e.g., thinking the negation of ∀x P(x) is ∀x ¬P(x), instead of the correct ∃x ¬P(x).
